ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operating_systems" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operating system */,
  "os_name" TEXT /* Name of the operating system */,
  "os_description" TEXT /* Description of the operating system */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the operating system */,
  "end_of_life_date" TEXT /* End-of-life date for the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client configuration */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client configuration */,
  "client_description" TEXT /* Description of the client configuration */,
  "client_type" TEXT /* Type of the client (e.g., native, third-party) */,
  "protocol_version" TEXT /* Version of the protocol used by the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server configuration */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server configuration */,
  "server_description" TEXT /* Description of the server configuration */,
  "server_type" TEXT /* Type of the server (e.g., web, database) */,
  "architecture" TEXT /* Server architecture (e.g., 32-bit, 64-bit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "native_clients" (
  "native_client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each native client entry */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the client configuration */,
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operating system */,
  "supported" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the native client is supported (true/false) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the native client support was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("native_client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_native_clients_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_native_clients_os_id FOREIGN KEY ("os_id") REFERENCES operating_systems ("os_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "third_party_contribs" (
  "contrib_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each third-party contribution entry */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the client configuration */,
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operating system */,
  "supported" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the third-party contribution is supported (true/false) */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Name of the vendor providing the contribution */,
  "license" TEXT /* License terms for the contribution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contrib_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_third_party_contribs_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_third_party_contribs_os_id FOREIGN KEY ("os_id") REFERENCES operating_systems ("os_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compatibility_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compatibility report */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the client configuration */,
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operating system */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server configuration */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the compatibility (e.g., Supported, Not Supported) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details about the compatibility issues or notes */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution for the compatibility issues (if any) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_reports_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_reports_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_reports_os_id FOREIGN KEY ("os_id") REFERENCES operating_systems ("os_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "support_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each support request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the request */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the compatibility report related to the request */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date when the request was made */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the support request */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the support request (e.g., Open, Closed) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the support request (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_support_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_support_requests_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES compatibility_reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Developer, Admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of permissions associated with this role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role association */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date when the action was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the log entry (e.g., System, Security, User) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operating system */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the client configuration */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server configuration */,
  "settings" TEXT /* Configuration settings in JSON format */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment type (e.g., Development, Production) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_os_id FOREIGN KEY ("os_id") REFERENCES operating_systems ("os_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Read status of the notification (true/false) */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., Email, In-App) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versioning" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration it relates to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Details of changes made in this version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when this version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when this version was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versioning_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environments" (
  "env_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment */,
  "env_name" TEXT /* Name of the environment */,
  "env_description" TEXT /* Description of the environment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("env_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environment_configs" (
  "env_config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment-configuration association */,
  "env_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the environment */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration assigned to the environment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("env_config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environment_configs_env_id FOREIGN KEY ("env_id") REFERENCES environments ("env_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environment_configs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vendor_info" (
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vendor */,
  "vendor_name" TEXT /* Name of the vendor */,
  "vendor_description" TEXT /* Description of the vendor */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the vendor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vendor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contrib_vendor_map" (
  "contrib_vendor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contribution-vendor association */,
  "contrib_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the third-party contribution */,
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the vendor providing the contribution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contrib_vendor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contrib_vendor_map_vendor_id FOREIGN KEY ("vendor_id") REFERENCES vendor_info ("vendor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contrib_vendor_map_contrib_id FOREIGN KEY ("contrib_id") REFERENCES third_party_contribs ("contrib_id")
)
